heroHeadline: UN: Gaza Ceasefire 'Increasingly Fragile' — 824 Killed Since October 2025; IDF Drone Kills 9-Year-Old in Khan Younis
heroSubtitle: UN Assistant Secretary-General Khiari told the Security Council on April 28 that the Gaza ceasefire is 'increasingly fragile,' with 824 Palestinians killed and 2,318 wounded since October 2025. On April 28, an IDF drone strike killed 9-year-old Adel Al-Najjar in eastern Khan Younis while he was collecting cardboard for cooking fuel — the IDF said it was targeting a militant near the Yellow Line and placed the incident under review. A separate Israeli airstrike in the Al-Rimal neighborhood of Gaza City killed a senior Hamas intelligence operative and three others. OHCHR on April 29 reported 92% of Gaza's housing has been destroyed, that continuous displacement amounts to 'forcible transfer' under international law, and that 589 aid workers and 294 journalists have been killed since October 7, 2023. Al Jazeera documented 925 West Bank movement obstacles — a 20-year high.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, IDF, Gaza Ministry of Health, B'Tselem, ICC, ICJ, Reuters, Al Jazeera, OHCHR, UNRWA, Euronews, Yesh Din, Human Rights Watch, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are subject to ongoing verification. The conflict has persisted since Israeli independence on May 14, 1948.
